title: Asuka and Death
wikitext:
[]: Asuka and Death             Quote Post by Gamer_2k4 » Wed Dec 02, 2009 2:22 am This came to me as I was driving today (the thought pattern that led to it is strange but explainable), and I don't recall it being addressed here, at least not recently. What the heck is up with Asuka and how she deals with her imminent death? If I recall correctly, Asuka came close to death three times in the series and movie. The first happened in Episode 10, when it seemed inevitable that Unit 02 would succumb to the heat and pressure of the magma. The second was in Episode 24, which Asuka (arguably) tries to commit suicide. The final time was in Episode 25', which of course was the "I don't want to die" scene. So here we have three different situations and three completely different reactions to death. Magma Diver Asuka reacts with calm resignation or even acceptance. We don't see her emotions prior to the suicide attempt, but judging from her relative passivity upon finding herself alive, I think it's safe to say she wasn't feeling especially emotional beforehand. In EoE, her mumbles turn to screams as she actively rejects death. So what's going on? Is the solution as simple as realizing her relative control in each of the situations? In the first case, there was absolutely nothing she personally could've done, granted. I suppose that makes her reaction a logical one, and perhaps even the right one. Also, if she's trying to commit suicide, then it's because she's reached the pits of despair, and her apathy makes total sense, especially given that she was in control of her fate the entire time. So everything's well and good, assuming those are the only two incidents. However, we still have the events of EoE to resolve. Asuka's still an emotional wreck there; she's essentially in the same state that she was in the episode before (24). What flipped the switch in her mind? Was the suicide attempt a failure because of that subconscious desire to stay alive? Was she able to turn things around in EoE because of her mother's comforting encouragement ("You mustn't die yet", "I won't let you die", etc.) combined with gradual realization that she could control her fate? But no...the whispered encouragement only came in response to Asuka's initial pleas of "I don't want to die." So you can see I haven't quite figured this out yet. Why does Asuka suddenly change her mind? How can those three events all logically fit together?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Wed Dec 02, 2009 2:32 am When she attempted suicide, she was at rock bottom and her only escape was to die. But this was in the spur of depressed emotions and she thought it felt right at the time. In EoE, however, she's more reacting to people trying to kill her. There's a difference between wanting to kill yourself and letting others murder you. So in this instance, she's more saying "I don't want to be killed." Had she not come to the realization that her mother had always been watching over her, and somehow survived the JSSDF attack (or it never happened in the first place) she might have attempted suicide again.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Dec 02, 2009 2:46 am EoE adjusts the characters from where they were in the series for dramatic effect. We've discussed the case of Shinji Ikari repeatedly; but the same argument could be made for Asuka too. Just staying as she was in ep 25 Asuka: I'm in Eva without wanting to be. I'm forced to be here. This junk won't move! Oh, no, it's me who's junk. I'm useless. No one needs me. No one needs a pilot who cannot pilot Eva.

[]:             Quote Post by Archon Eva » Wed Dec 02, 2009 3:39 am I think it's because she had no control over what was happening, she was used to having control over herself and her emotions. When her mother died she had some control of what she was feeling, so she had lost control and figured herself useless. Death was the only escape because she couldn't control her life anymore.

[]:             Quote Post by DIDDY » Wed Dec 02, 2009 4:25 am I should probably be in bed instead of posting but... I don't think the three events are not irreconciliable. One's attitude towards life and death can vary (greatly/frequently) over time. Your fear of death could remain constant at all times, but you could have the courage or impulse to off yourself one day and not the other. At some point, you could also be less afraid of death than you are of life (for whatever reason - in Asuka's case in ep. 24, probably feeling unworthy of anything that makes life bearable). Anyway, vary these factors and you may want to die one day, and not the other. I guess that leaves the Magmadiver bit as the odd one out. I think you'd have to take into account that Asuka in ep. 10 had not yet had all her defenses torn down by Arael and a series of failures (real or perceived), such as not having the highest sync rate anymore, losing to Zeruel, losing to Shinji, losing to Rei, losing to Misato (Kaji), etc. In ep. 10, Asuka had much more psychological strength to dedicate to staying composed and to convince herself that it was "alright". I might be pushing this a little too much, but in a way, after years of acting strong/independant, I suppose it might have become some sort of habit for Asuka to deny she feels any fear at all, especially in front of others (I don't think she'd even admit it to herself at this point in ep. 10, but if I remember correctly, the whole team was monitoring her). As the series progresses past ep. 10, and as Asuka found less and less signs that people didn't think she was as worthless as she thought she was, she eventually stopped thinking it was any use to keep the act up. You could also say that post-Arael Asuka is much more in touch with all the aspects of her inner self than pre-Arael Asuka is (after all, Arael forced Asuka into introspection), so she really could not deny her real self anymore. Anyway, all in all, I think she feared death all along and that she never really accepted it. Her attitude/behavior changes seem very realistic to me. Then again, maybe I'm completely confused. Or maybe Anno didn't give ep. 10 much thought. EDIT: And btw Gamer_2k4, I love your avatar

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Wed Dec 02, 2009 2:02 pm Magma diver = "Shit, nothing I can do. This sucks, yo, I'm literally helpless. But atleast I killed the Angel. I could be a big damn hero, and...Shinji OMG THANKS!" Suicide attempt = "Got mind raped. Can't pilot. Worthless. Sort of realizing my bitchiness has forced away everyone I know. Cross the street, not down the highway." EoE = "Wait, what the fuck?! Don't kill me! I want my death to be on my own terms! I won't let you! I'm...I'm piloting! And my momma's here! Momma's always been here! All I ever needed was Momma, and I won't let you tear us apart! If Momma's here I'm worth something! I can keep fighting!"

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Wed Dec 02, 2009 3:06 pm AuraTwilight wrote: Magma diver = "Shit, nothing I can do. This sucks, yo, I'm literally helpless. But atleast I killed the Angel. I could be a big damn hero, and...Shinji OMG THANKS!" Suicide attempt = "Got mind raped. Can't pilot. Worthless. Sort of realizing my bitchiness has forced away everyone I know. Cross the street, not down the highway." EoE = "Wait, what the fuck?! Don't kill me! I want my death to be on my own terms! I won't let you! I'm...I'm piloting! And my momma's here! []: Re: Asuka and Death             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Dec 02, 2009 6:50 pm Gamer_2k4 wrote: Is the solution as simple as realizing her relative control in each of the situations?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Dec 02, 2009 11:54 pm Ironically, Asuka is repeating "I don't want to die" to herself before and during Kyoko's "You won't die, please die with me!" (Kyoko says "Die with me!" twice) rant... And then they do end up dying together.
